ogTitle: Wilsons Promontory vs Wimbledon Heights: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Wilsons Promontory and Wimbledon Heights.
heading: Wilsons Promontory vs Wimbledon Heights — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Wilsons Promontory, Victoria and Wimbledon Heights, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Wilsons Promontory has a lower overall climate risk score (42/100) compared to Wimbledon Heights (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Wilsons Promontory or Wimbledon Heights safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Wilsons Promontory has a flood risk score of 49/100, while Wimbledon Heights scores 58/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Wilsons Promontory scores 46/100 for bushfire risk. Wimbledon Heights scores 52/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Wilsons Promontory or Wimbledon Heights?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Wilsons Promontory has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.1% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
